Region concentration in East US for relational services: 71% of SQL servers and 80% of SQL databases are in eastus (and eastus is also a common location for VNets and storage), so deploy the finance web app and its database in eastus to minimize latency and align with existing operational regions.

Existing SQL database sizing skews to Hyperscale Gen5 with small vCore counts: 60% of databases use the Hyperscale tier and HS_Gen5 appears frequently with capacity=2, so plan the finance DB using the tenant's Gen5/Hyperscale sizing pattern (or explicitly justify a GeneralPurpose smaller tier) to match operational expectations and capacity planning.

Public network access is commonly left enabled for secrets and databases: key vaults have publicnetworkaccess Enabled for ~92% and SQL servers are Enabled for ~57%, indicating a tendency toward internet-accessible endpoints, so enforce private access for the finance app (disable publicNetworkAccess for SQL and Key Vault and use private endpoints) to meet internal-only access requirements.

Private networking primitives are present and paired with DNS: there are 56 private endpoints and 74 private DNS zones and frequent itemsets show NSGs+VNets+private endpoints together, so implement a private endpoint + private DNS zone for the finance SQL and Key Vault and secure them with subnet-level NSGs to align with tenant networking patterns.

User-assigned managed identities are widely used: 370 userAssignedIdentities exist and they appear in many frequent resource group patterns, so provision a user-assigned managed identity for the app to authenticate to SQL and Key Vault instead of embedding credentials.

Web traffic protection commonly uses WAF-capable gateways: all recorded Application Gateways use WAF_v2 and Front Door WAF policies use Premium_AzureFrontDoor, so design the internal finance web front-end behind an internal Application Gateway or WAF-enabled edge that matches tenant WAF usage and security posture.

Monitoring and log integration is standard practice: 549 Application Insights components and 149 Log Analytics workspaces are present with ingestion enabled, so include Application Insights instrumentation for the web app and forward logs/metrics to a Log Analytics workspace for alerting and compliance evidence.

Key Vaults follow safe retention but limited hardening: enablesoftdelete is True for ~98.8% but enablepurgeprotection is True for only ~8.9% and enablerbacauthorization is False for ~60%, so for finance secrets enable purge protection, enable RBAC for vault operations, and restrict vault network access to meet stricter secret governance.

Virtual network and subnet naming shows a mix of default and app-specific subnets: ~40% of VNets retain a 'default' subnet while ~11% include an 'appSubnet', so create explicit app and db subnets (avoiding the default where possible) and apply dedicated NSGs and route controls for the finance application architecture.
